title: Exacum viscosum
intro: Exacum viscosum, also known as sticky gentian or sticky exacum, is a plant species in the gentian family (Gentianaceae) characterized by its sticky leaves and stems. The health benefits of Exacum viscosum include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Exacum viscosum include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Exacum viscosum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules.
taxonomy: Exacum viscosum belongs to the family Gentianaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical Asia, specifically in countries such as India and Sri Lanka. The plant is further classified as a member of the genus Exacum, which consists of a small number of species. Exacum viscosum is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial with sticky leaves and small white flowers. The plant's classification into the family Gentianaceae suggests a close relationship with other plants in the same family, such as the gentian.
